description_long: This is a large, irregularly shaped stone with a predominantly angular form and rough, jagged edges. Its surface is mostly a pale, off-white color with subtle gray veining and mottled patches, giving it a natural, unpolished appearance. The stone's faces vary in texture, with some sides appearing more planar and others more rugged and fractured. The overall mass and density suggest it is composed of a hard, durable rock such as marble or limestone. There are no visible artificial modifications, carvings, or signs of human intervention. The stone's size and form make it suitable as a natural barrier or obstacle in an urban park, roadside, or landscaped area.
description: A large, angular pale stone with rough, jagged surfaces and subtle gray veining, resembling natural marble or limestone. Its irregular shape and unpolished texture make it a prominent obstacle or barrier.
description_view_0: This view highlights a broad, slightly sloped face with subtle gray veining and a rough, fractured lower edge.
description_view_1: This angle shows a more rectangular, blocky side with pronounced surface texture and visible mottling.
description_view_2: This perspective reveals a smoother, lighter face with minimal veining and a gently curved edge.
description_view_3: This view displays a combination of angular and planar surfaces, with a prominent jagged edge and visible shadowing.
attribute_location: urban park, roadside verge, landscaped plaza
attribute_placement: set as a standalone feature or barrier on the ground
attribute_pattern: Subtle gray veining and mottled patches, irregular and naturally occurring, with no artificial carvings.
attribute_weathering: Rough and unpolished, with minor surface abrasions and no significant signs of lichen or deep erosion, suggesting moderate exposure to the elements.
attribute_integration_with_surroundings: Contrasts with paved or manicured urban surfaces, but can blend with natural landscaping or other stone features; may be partially surrounded by grass or soil.
